baseVersion: v2 complete as of S160 on branch ralph/luminary-v2

v3Philosophy.theme: Notes become first-class knowledge citizens: organized into typed collections, connected to the entity graph, and discoverable through semantic clustering and hierarchical tags.
principles: Notes are graph nodes, not flat records. Every note saved fires an async task that upserts a Kuzu NOTE node and edges to Entity nodes via GLiNER extraction. The graph is the connective tissue; SQL is the authoritative store.

Hierarchical tags with a controlled vocabulary. Tags follow a slash-convention (science/biology/genetics). A shadow index table (NoteTagIndexModel) makes prefix-search O(1) and enables hierarchy-aware filtering. Tag normalization via embedding similarity prevents vocabulary explosion.

Collections replace group_name. Notes belong to zero or many named collections (many-to-many). Collections have metadata (description, color) and support 2-level nesting. Semantic clustering (HDBSCAN) auto-suggests collections from embedding similarity -- human approves, machine proposes.

Performance contracts are explicit. The 1024-dim vector schema must match the embedding model (bge-m3). Tag index queries must pass p95 < 100ms at 1,000 notes. Every new async path follows the established asyncio.create_task fire-and-forget pattern with explicit error logging.

Cross-tab navigation is event-driven. Clicking a tag node in the Viz tab fires a 'luminary:navigate' DOM event; App.tsx routes to Notes tab with the tag pre-applied. No URL hacks, no shared global state beyond Zustand store.
Every new service method and every new API endpoint must have at least one pytest test. No exceptions (inherited from v2 philosophy).

Phase 2 seed (post Phase 1): note-to-note bidirectional links (S171), notes visible in Knowledge graph (S172), collection health reports (S173), export to Obsidian vault and Anki (S174), multi-document notes (S175). Phase 2 begins only after all Phase 1 stories pass and docs/v3-phase1-completion.md validation checklist is fully checked.

stories.title: Note collections: schema, API, and group_name migration
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: NoteModel.group_name is a freeform string giving each note exactly one optional group label with no metadata, no nesting, and no many-to-many support. As notes grow past a few dozen, users need named collections with descriptions and colors, the ability to assign one note to multiple collections, and optional parent-child nesting (e.g., Learning > Physics > Quantum). group_name is deprecated in this story but kept as a nullable column for backward compatibility. USER JOURNEY: User has 80 notes scattered across 12 distinct group_name values -> opens Notes tab -> sees new 'Collections' sidebar section -> clicks 'Create collection' -> creates 'Quantum Mechanics' under parent collection 'Physics' -> assigns existing notes via checkbox panel -> sidebar shows 'Physics (45)' expandable to 'Quantum Mechanics (18)'. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: None from prior Phase 13 stories. NoteModel.group_name (S87 NoteEditorDialog) preserved as nullable -- do not remove the column. GET /notes groups endpoint (S89) remains functional; add collection_id filter alongside. BACKEND: NoteCollectionModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), name (str required), description (str nullable), color (str hex default '#6366F1'), icon (str nullable, emoji), parent_collection_id (FK to self nullable), sort_order (int default 0), created_at, updated_at. NoteCollectionMemberModel pivot: note_id (cascade delete), collection_id (cascade delete), added_at, composite PK (note_id, collection_id). db_init.py migration: CREATE TABLE note_collections; CREATE TABLE note_collection_members; SELECT DISTINCT group_name FROM notes WHERE group_name IS NOT NULL; INSERT INTO note_collections(id, name, color) for each distinct value using a generated UUID; INSERT INTO note_collection_members SELECT id, <new_collection_id> FROM notes WHERE group_name = <value>. Router routers/collections.py (new file): GET /collections (flat list with note_count), GET /collections/tree (nested JSON, children array), POST /collections, PUT /collections/{id}, DELETE /collections/{id} (cascades members, not notes), POST /collections/{id}/notes (add note, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), DELETE /collections/{id}/notes/{note_id}. Nesting constraint: POST /collections returns 422 when the requested parent_collection_id itself has a parent (max depth = 2). GET /notes gains optional collection_id query param: adds JOIN note_collection_members WHERE collection_id = :cid. Register router in main.py. CollectionSchema TypedDict: id, name, description, color, icon, parent_collection_id, sort_order, note_count, children (tree endpoint only). TESTS: Unit test: collection at depth 3 returns 422. Unit test: DELETE /collections/{id} cascades pivot rows; notes survive. Unit test: migration inserts one collection per distinct group_name with correct membership. Unit test: GET /notes?collection_id={id} returns only notes in that collection via pivot join.

stories.title: Hierarchical tags: shadow index table, canonical tag registry, prefix-search API
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Tags are stored as flat JSON arrays in notes.tags with no canonical registry, no hierarchy, and no SQL index -- tag filtering via json_each(notes.tags) is O(n) over the full notes table. PKM research on 8,000-note vaults confirms flat tag systems break at scale: synonym explosion ('ml', 'machine-learning', 'Machine Learning') and lack of browse hierarchy (clicking 'science' should match 'science/biology'). This story adds: (1) NoteTagIndexModel -- a shadow/denorm table that explodes each note's tags into indexed rows for O(1) lookup; (2) CanonicalTagModel -- a registry supporting slash-convention parent-child hierarchy ('science/biology/genetics'); (3) a full tag API including prefix-search autocomplete; (4) hierarchical matching in GET /notes?tag= (query for 'science' also returns 'science/biology' and 'science/physics/quantum' notes). USER JOURNEY: User types 'prog' in tag filter -> autocomplete shows 'programming', 'programming/python', 'programming/go' -> clicks 'programming' -> all programming/* notes shown in main pane. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: GET /notes tag filter (S89) rewritten to use NoteTagIndexModel prefix query. Note create/update/delete routes (S89) must call _sync_tag_index synchronously. The existing suggest-tags endpoint (S89) returns flat strings; S162 registers them into CanonicalTagModel on upsert. BACKEND: NoteTagIndexModel in models.py: note_id (str, no FK -- shadow table to avoid FK overhead), tag_full (str e.g. 'science/biology'), tag_root (str first segment 'science'), tag_parent (str all-but-last segment 'science', empty string if top-level). Unique composite on (note_id, tag_full). CanonicalTagModel: id = tag_full slug (PK), display_name (str), parent_tag (str nullable, FK to self), note_count (int denorm), created_at. TagAliasModel: alias (str PK), canonical_tag_id (FK). Sync function _sync_tag_index(note_id, tags, db) in routers/notes.py: called synchronously on note create/update/delete. On create/update: DELETE FROM note_tag_index WHERE note_id = :nid; INSERT for each tag in tags; UPDATE CanonicalTagModel note_count (+1 for new, -1 for removed). On delete: DELETE all rows for note_id; decrement counts. NOTE: _sync_tag_index is called synchronously within the same DB write transaction -- do NOT use asyncio.create_task here. Use Semaphore(1) if concurrent note writes are ever added. Router routers/tags.py (new file): GET /tags (flat list sorted note_count DESC, fields: id, display_name, note_count, parent_tag), GET /tags/tree (nested with children), GET /tags/{tag_id}/notes (notes with this tag including children), GET /tags/autocomplete?q= (LIKE :q || '%' on NoteTagIndexModel.tag_full, up to 10 results), POST /tags (create canonical), PUT /tags/{tag_id} (rename display_name or re-parent), DELETE /tags/{tag_id} (409 if note_count > 0). GET /notes?tag= rewritten: WHERE note_id IN (SELECT note_id FROM note_tag_index WHERE tag_full = :tag OR tag_full LIKE :tag || '/%'). Register router in main.py. db_init.py migration: CREATE TABLE note_tag_index; CREATE TABLE canonical_tags; CREATE TABLE tag_aliases; CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_tag_index_tag_full ON note_tag_index(tag_full); CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_tag_index_note_id ON note_tag_index(note_id); backfill: SELECT id, tags FROM notes; call _sync logic in pure SQL for existing rows. TESTS: Unit test: note with tags ['programming/python', 'programming/go'] produces 2 NoteTagIndexModel rows with tag_root='programming'. Unit test: GET /notes?tag=programming returns notes tagged 'programming', 'programming/python', 'programming/go'. Unit test: deleting a note removes its NoteTagIndexModel rows and decrements CanonicalTagModel.note_count. Unit test: autocomplete for 'prog' returns 'programming' and its children sorted by note_count.

stories.title: Notes as Kuzu graph nodes: NOTE type, WRITTEN_ABOUT entity edges, TAG_IS_CONCEPT bridge
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Notes exist entirely outside the Kuzu knowledge graph. The only bridge is the transient chat notes_node which retrieves note text via NoteSearchService at query time -- there are no persistent graph edges from notes to entities. This means the Viz knowledge graph shows no notes, note discovery through graph traversal is impossible, and queries like 'find all notes mentioning entities related to Gradient Descent' require a full-text scan instead of a graph traversal. This story adds three structural additions to Kuzu: NOTE node type, WRITTEN_ABOUT edges (NOTE -> Entity, auto-extracted via GLiNER on note save), and TAG_IS_CONCEPT edges (NOTE -> Entity, when a tag matches an existing Entity.name). DERIVED_FROM edges (NOTE -> Document) link notes to source books. USER JOURNEY: User saves a note about 'backpropagation' while reading a deep learning book -> async background task runs GLiNER, finds existing Entity 'Backpropagation' in Kuzu, creates WRITTEN_ABOUT edge; tag 'neural-networks' matches Entity 'neural-networks', creates TAG_IS_CONCEPT edge -> user opens Viz tab -> note node appears connected to both entities. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: POST /notes, PUT /notes/{id}, DELETE /notes/{id} (S89) each gain an asyncio.create_task call (upsert or delete). chat_graph.py notes_node (S79) upgraded: after NoteSearchService.search(), enriches context snippets with Kuzu entity names via get_entities_for_note(). Kuzu schema adds 3 edge tables and 1 node table to existing graph (S77 introduced the schema). BACKEND: graph.py _create_schema() additions: NODE TABLE Note(id STRING, note_id STRING, preview STRING, created_at STRING) PRIMARY KEY(id). REL TABLE WRITTEN_ABOUT(FROM Note TO Entity) {confidence: FLOAT}. REL TABLE TAG_IS_CONCEPT(FROM Note TO Entity) {tag: STRING}. REL TABLE DERIVED_FROM(FROM Note TO Document). NoteGraphService in app/services/note_graph.py: async upsert_note_node(note_id, content, document_id, tags, db): (1) Upsert Note node (id=note_id, preview=content[:200], created_at=now ISO string). (2) If document_id: upsert DERIVED_FROM edge to Document node. (3) Run GLiNER on content for PERSON, ORGANIZATION, CONCEPT, TECHNOLOGY entity types. For each extracted entity: run Kuzu query to look up Entity by name (case-insensitive LOWER() comparison); guard with has_next() before get_next() -- do not call get_next() without has_next() check (Kuzu raises on empty result). If found: upsert WRITTEN_ABOUT edge with confidence = entity_score. (4) For each tag: Kuzu lookup Entity where LOWER(name) = LOWER(tag); if found: upsert TAG_IS_CONCEPT edge. Called from notes router as asyncio.create_task (fire-and-forget, same pattern as vector embeddings). Wrap the task body in try/except and log errors via app logger -- fire-and-forget must never fail silently. async delete_note_node(note_id): DELETE FROM Note WHERE note_id = :nid (Kuzu cascades incident edges on node delete). Called synchronously in DELETE /notes/{id} handler before note row deletion. async get_entities_for_note(note_id): Cypher MATCH (n:Note)-[r:WRITTEN_ABOUT|TAG_IS_CONCEPT]->(e:Entity) WHERE n.note_id = $nid RETURN e.name, e.type, r.confidence, type(r). Guard each get_next() with has_next(). async get_notes_for_entity(entity_name): MATCH (n:Note)-[:WRITTEN_ABOUT|:TAG_IS_CONCEPT]->(e:Entity) WHERE LOWER(e.name) = LOWER($name) RETURN n.note_id. GET /notes/{note_id}/entities: calls get_entities_for_note, returns [{name, type, confidence, edge_type}]. chat_graph.py notes_node: after NoteSearchService results, for top-3 results call get_entities_for_note(note_id) and append entity names to context chunk metadata. TESTS: Unit test: upsert_note_node for note whose content matches an existing Entity creates WRITTEN_ABOUT edge; entity absent from Kuzu is skipped without error. Unit test: note with document_id creates DERIVED_FROM edge to correct Document node. Unit test: tag matching existing Entity.name creates TAG_IS_CONCEPT edge. Unit test: delete_note_node removes Note node; subsequent get_entities_for_note returns []. Unit test: get_notes_for_entity returns correct note_id for a known entity.
acceptanceCriteria: Kuzu schema in graph.py: Note node table (id, note_id, preview, created_at), WRITTEN_ABOUT rel (Note->Entity, confidence FLOAT), TAG_IS_CONCEPT rel (Note->Entity, tag STRING), DERIVED_FROM rel (Note->Document)

stories.title: Notes performance refactor: fix vector dimension mismatch (384->1024), add tag indexes, batch reindex
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Three technical debts in the notes subsystem must be resolved before Phase 1 work scales to large note corpora. (1) VECTOR DIM BUG: The LanceDB note_vectors_v2 table schema is defined with pa.list_(pa.float32(), 384) in vector_store.py but BAAI/bge-m3 produces 1024-dim embeddings -- a silent mismatch that causes vector upserts to fail or produce truncated vectors, making semantic note search unreliable. (2) TAG QUERY INDEXES: S162 added NoteTagIndexModel with indexes in db_init.py. This story adds a @pytest.mark.slow performance regression guard to verify those indexes hold at 1,000-note scale. (3) ORPHANED EMBEDDINGS: Notes created before the vector store was initialized, or during Ollama downtime, have no embedding; there is no way to identify or backfill them without a dedicated endpoint. USER JOURNEY: Admin (developer) calls POST /admin/notes/reindex -> {queued: True, total_notes: 247} -> background task re-embeds 31 notes with missing or mismatched vectors -> GET /notes/search now returns semantically relevant results for all notes. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: vector_store.py (S89) is modified -- existing notes may have 384-dim vectors that will be erased by the migration; re-embedding all notes is required. NoteTagIndexModel indexes (S162) are verified under load here. BACKEND: NOTE_VECTOR_DIM = 1024 constant in vector_store.py. _get_or_create_note_table(): inspect existing LanceDB table schema (table.schema, check the vector field list_size via PyArrow field type). If list_size != 1024: log warning 'note_vectors_v2 schema mismatch (found {actual_dim}-dim) -- dropping and recreating with 1024-dim', call tbl.drop() (or the LanceDB equivalent for table deletion -- use db.drop_table('note_vectors_v2') via the lancedb connection object), then recreate. This detection runs at startup (called from get_vector_store()). ReindexService in app/services/reindex_service.py: async reindex_notes(db: AsyncSession) -> ReindexReport TypedDict {total: int, reindexed: int, failed: int}: fetch all NoteModel rows (id, content). For each: check if note_id present in LanceDB (synchronous tbl.search(query_vector).where(f"note_id = '{nid}'").limit(1).to_pandas() -- wrap sync call in asyncio.to_thread). If absent: re-embed content and upsert. Catch embedding exceptions per-note; increment failed. Return report. Run entire reindex as asyncio.create_task. POST /admin/notes/reindex: X-Admin-Key header checked against settings.ADMIN_KEY (env var, default empty string = no auth for local dev). If ADMIN_KEY non-empty and header missing or wrong: return 403. Returns {queued: True, total_notes: int}. PERFORMANCE TEST (@pytest.mark.slow, guarded by LUMINARY_PERF_TESTS env var per patterns.md): insert 1,000 synthetic NoteModel + NoteTagIndexModel rows via fixture. Run GET /notes?tag=science 10 times via test client. Assert p95 < 100ms (use time.perf_counter). TESTS: Unit test: _get_or_create_note_table with a mock 384-dim schema triggers drop-and-recreate warning. Unit test: reindex_notes identifies notes absent from LanceDB and returns correct counts in ReindexReport. Unit test: POST /admin/notes/reindex with wrong X-Admin-Key returns 403 when ADMIN_KEY is set to a non-empty value.

stories.title: Hierarchical tag browser UI: tree sidebar, tag management panel, NoteEditorDialog autocomplete
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: S162 adds the hierarchical tag backend (NoteTagIndexModel, CanonicalTagModel, prefix-search API). This story builds the frontend counterpart: a hierarchical tag tree in the Notes sidebar, a tag management panel (rename, re-parent, merge), and an upgraded NoteEditorDialog tag input with hierarchy-aware autocomplete. The current flat chip input (S87) does not communicate hierarchy to the user; a tag like 'programming/python' is entered and displayed as an opaque string. After this story the UI navigates the hierarchy visually and helps users stay within the controlled vocabulary. USER JOURNEY: User opens Notes sidebar -> 'Tags' section shows 'programming (34)' collapsible -> expands to 'python (18)', 'go (9)', 'rust (7)' -> clicks 'programming' -> all programming/* notes displayed -> hovers 'ml' tag -> gear icon -> 'Merge into...' combobox -> types 'machine-learning' -> accepts -> 34 notes updated. stories.title: Tag co-occurrence graph in Viz tab: Sigma.js tag network mode with cross-tab note filter
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: The Viz tab renders the book entity graph via Sigma.js v3 + Graphology but shows no note-derived structure. This story adds a 'Tags' graph mode: nodes are canonical tags (sized by note_count, colored by parent tag family), edges are co-occurrences (two tags appearing on the same note, weighted by co-occurrence count). The graph reveals how the user's personal knowledge base clusters -- distinct from the entity graph which reflects book content. Clicking a tag node fires a cross-tab navigation event that activates the Notes tab with that tag applied as a filter. The graph is generated from NoteTagIndexModel (S162) with a cap of 200 nodes and 500 edges for rendering clarity. USER JOURNEY: User opens Viz tab -> clicks 'Tags' mode -> tag network renders: large 'machine-learning' node connected to 'neural-networks', 'optimization', 'backpropagation'; separate cluster 'history' -> 'ancient-rome', 'empire' -> user clicks 'machine-learning' -> Notes tab activates with tag filter applied, showing all ml/* notes. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Viz tab mode selector (S112 added entity graph modes) gains a new 'Tags' button. S162 NoteTagIndexModel is the data source. Notes page activeTag (S165 useAppStore field) is the cross-tab target. BACKEND: TagGraphService in app/services/tag_graph.py: async build_tag_graph(db: AsyncSession) -> TagGraphData TypedDict {nodes: list[TagNode], edges: list[TagEdge], generated_at: str}. TagNode: {id, label, note_count, parent_tag, color}. TagEdge: {source, target, weight}. Implementation: (1) Fetch top-200 CanonicalTagModel by note_count where note_count > 0. (2) Co-occurrence SQL: SELECT a.tag_full, b.tag_full, COUNT(*) as weight FROM note_tag_index a JOIN note_tag_index b ON a.note_id = b.note_id AND a.tag_full < b.tag_full WHERE a.tag_full IN (:top_200) AND b.tag_full IN (:top_200) GROUP BY 1, 2 HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY weight DESC LIMIT 500. (3) Node color: deterministic from parent_tag -- hash parent_tag string to index 0-11, map to 12-color palette (hex constants in service). Nodes with no parent use a default grey. In-memory LRU invalidation: TagGraphService caches the result dict; cache is cleared in _sync_tag_index whenever any tag row is modified. GET /tags/graph: calls build_tag_graph; returns TagGraphData. TypedDicts in app/types/. FRONTEND: Viz tab: add 'Tags' button to mode selector. On Sigma node click: dispatch new CustomEvent('luminary:navigate', { detail: { tab: 'notes', tagFilter: nodeId } }) -- the tagFilter field maps to the activeTag field in useAppStore. stories.title: Smart tag normalization: embedding similarity scan, merge suggestions, accept/reject drawer
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Even with hierarchical tags (S162), users accumulate synonymous tags across notes from different books and sessions: 'ml', 'machine-learning', 'Machine Learning', 'AI', 'artificial-intelligence'. The LLM tagger (S89) generates new tags per-note without checking the existing controlled vocabulary. PKM research on 8,000-note vaults found 2,141 unique tags -- roughly 1 per 4 notes -- an unmanageable vocabulary that defeats the purpose of tagging. This story adds a SmartTagNormalizerService that embeds all canonical tag names using bge-m3, finds semantically similar pairs above a 0.85 cosine similarity threshold, and surfaces merge suggestions in a NormalizationDrawer where the user reviews and accepts or rejects each pair. USER JOURNEY: User clicks 'Normalize' (wrench icon) in Notes sidebar Tags section -> scan runs -> drawer opens: 6 suggestions including 'ml -- machine-learning (91% similar, 34 notes)' -> user accepts 4, rejects 2 -> 62 notes updated with canonical tags. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: POST /tags/merge (S165) is reused by the accept action. TagAliasModel and CanonicalTagModel (S162) are the target schema. _sync_tag_index (S162) is called by merge for each updated note. The NoteTagIndexModel cache invalidation (S167 TagGraphService) must be triggered by the merge operation. BACKEND: TagMergeSuggestionModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), tag_a_id (str, FK CanonicalTagModel.id), tag_b_id (str), similarity (float), suggested_canonical_id (str -- whichever of the pair has higher note_count), status ('pending'|'accepted'|'rejected'), created_at. db_init.py: CREATE TABLE tag_merge_suggestions. SmartTagNormalizerService in app/services/tag_normalizer.py: async scan(db) -> int: fetch all CanonicalTagModel rows. Embed display_names using the same embedding model as the retriever -- call the embeddings function used in NoteSearchService (e.g. embed a list of strings via the ONNX bge-m3 session, same path as chunk/note embedding). Uses the same bge-m3 ONNX model as the retriever (1024-dim, requires S170 to have run first to ensure note_vectors_v2 is 1024-dim). Call asyncio.to_thread for embedding calls. Compute pairwise cosine similarity as numpy dot product on L2-normalised vectors. For pairs with similarity > 0.85 where neither is an alias of the other (check TagAliasModel): insert TagMergeSuggestionModel row; suggested_canonical = whichever has higher note_count. Return count of new suggestions created. POST /tags/normalization/scan: triggers scan as asyncio.create_task; returns {queued: True}. GET /tags/normalization/suggestions: returns pending TagMergeSuggestionModel rows, each with tag_a {id, display_name, note_count} and tag_b {id, display_name, note_count} expanded. POST /tags/normalization/suggestions/{id}/accept: calls existing POST /tags/merge logic (reuse, not duplicate) with tag_a_id and suggested_canonical_id; sets status=accepted; returns {affected_notes: int}. POST /tags/normalization/suggestions/{id}/reject: sets status=rejected. FRONTEND: Tags section header in Notes sidebar: 'Normalize' wrench icon button. On click: POST /tags/normalization/scan; show spinner; poll GET /tags/normalization/suggestions every 2s until suggestions arrive; open NormalizationDrawer (Sheet from right). NormalizationDrawer: each suggestion row shows TagA chip -- TagB chip, similarity badge (e.g. '91%'), '{n} notes affected'. stories.title: Semantic note clustering via HDBSCAN: auto-suggest collections from embedding similarity
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Note collections (S161) require manual curation -- users must create collections and assign notes themselves. At scale this is a maintenance burden. PKM research confirms that at 1,000+ notes automated organization is non-negotiable. This story adds a semantic clustering service using HDBSCAN (Hierarchical Density-Based Spatial Clustering) over note embeddings from LanceDB note_vectors_v2, generates collection name suggestions via LLM, and surfaces them in a 'Suggested Collections' sidebar panel. HDBSCAN is preferred over k-means because it handles variable cluster sizes, does not require pre-specifying cluster count, and marks noise notes (label=-1) as unclustered rather than forcing them into a group. The note_vectors_v2 table now uses 1024-dim vectors (S170 fixed the schema) so clustering produces accurate semantic groups. USER JOURNEY: User has 120 notes with minimal manual organization -> clicks 'Auto-organize' in Notes sidebar -> system clusters notes -> 7 suggested collections: 'Quantum Mechanics (18 notes)', 'Machine Learning (34 notes)', 'Ancient History (12 notes)' etc. -> user reviews each with 3-note preview -> accepts 5 -> 5 new NoteCollections created with notes assigned. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Depends on S161 (NoteCollectionModel + NoteCollectionMemberModel created by accept). Depends on S170 (1024-dim note_vectors_v2 -- clustering only runs if vectors exist). S164 CollectionTree must refresh after accept to show new collections. BACKEND: scikit-learn >= 1.3 in pyproject.toml (uses sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN added in scikit-learn 1.3 -- no extra hdbscan package needed). ClusterSuggestionModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), suggested_name (str), note_ids (JSON list of str), confidence_score (float, mean pairwise cosine within cluster), status ('pending'|'accepted'|'rejected'), created_at. db_init.py: CREATE TABLE cluster_suggestions. ClusteringService in app/services/clustering_service.py: async cluster_notes(db) -> int: (1) Fetch all rows from LanceDB note_vectors_v2 via await asyncio.to_thread(table.to_pandas); extract note_id and vector columns. (2) Build numpy matrix from vectors. If fewer than 6 notes with vectors: return 0 (not enough data). (3) Run sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN(min_cluster_size=3, min_samples=2, metric='cosine').fit_predict(matrix). (4) For each label != -1: gather member note_ids; compute centroid; select up to 3 medoid notes (closest to centroid by cosine distance). (5) Compute confidence_score = mean pairwise cosine similarity for cluster members. (6) LLM name generation: build prompt with up to 3 note excerpts (content[:150] each). LiteLLM call: system='Given these note excerpts, suggest a 2-4 word collection name capturing their common theme. Reply with the name only.'; user=excerpts. (7) Insert ClusterSuggestionModel row. Return count of new suggestions. Rate-limit: if a ClusterSuggestionModel with status=pending exists and created_at > now - 1 hour: skip clustering; return -1. POST /notes/cluster: if rate-limited (returns -1): return {cached: True, last_run: datetime ISO str}. Else triggers cluster_notes as asyncio.create_task; returns {queued: True, total_notes: int}. GET /notes/cluster/suggestions: returns pending ClusterSuggestionModel rows sorted by confidence_score DESC; each row includes first 3 note content previews (first 100 chars of each note content fetched by note_ids). POST /notes/cluster/suggestions/{id}/accept: create NoteCollectionModel(name=suggested_name, color='#6366F1'), bulk INSERT NoteCollectionMemberModel for all note_ids, set status=accepted; return {collection_id}. POST /notes/cluster/suggestions/{id}/reject: set status=rejected. FRONTEND: Notes sidebar: 'Suggested Collections' section visible only when GET /notes/cluster/suggestions returns pending items. 'Auto-organize' button (Wand2 icon) at top of Collections section triggers POST /notes/cluster; shows spinner while queued. Each suggestion card: suggested name, '{n} notes' badge, confidence bar (filled based on confidence_score), 3 note excerpt previews as muted text. Accept (Plus icon) and Reject (X icon) buttons. On accept: POST /suggestions/{id}/accept; invalidate GET /collections/tree; suggestion card animates out. STATES: Scanning: spinner on Auto-organize button. No suggestions: 'Auto-organize' button remains visible with no panel below. Accepted suggestion: collection appears in CollectionTree (cache invalidation). TESTS: Unit test: HDBSCAN with 6-vector fixture (3 closely similar + 3 dissimilar) produces exactly 1 cluster with correct member note_ids. stories.title: Note-to-note bidirectional links: explicit Zettelkasten-style connections
stories.phase: V3 Phase 2 - Note Intelligence and Synthesis
stories.description: CONTEXT: Phase 1 connects notes to entities (WRITTEN_ABOUT) and to documents (DERIVED_FROM) in Kuzu, but notes cannot reference each other directly. Zettelkasten research shows that explicit note-to-note links -- where each link carries a typed relationship -- are the primary driver of emergent knowledge structure at scale. Without them, related notes can only be discovered via shared tags or shared entities; the user cannot express 'this note elaborates on that note', 'this note contradicts that note', or 'see also'. This story adds a NoteLink model and a LINKS_TO Kuzu edge type, a link-creation UI in the note detail view, and bidirectional backlink display in NoteEditorDialog. USER JOURNEY: User is editing a note on 'Backpropagation' -> types '[[' to trigger note link autocomplete -> types 'grad' -> dropdown shows 'Gradient Descent' and 'Gradient Tape' notes -> selects 'Gradient Descent' with link type 'elaborates' -> the note gains an outgoing link chip 'elaborates -> Gradient Descent' -> Gradient Descent note's editor now shows a backlink 'backlinked by: Backpropagation (elaborates)'. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: NoteEditorDialog (S87, S164, S165) gains [[link]] trigger inside the content textarea. Notes as Kuzu nodes (S163) -- LINKS_TO edge type added to the same graph schema. GET /notes/{id} must return outgoing link_ids and incoming backlink_ids. BACKEND: NoteLinkModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), source_note_id (FK notes.id cascade delete), target_note_id (FK notes.id cascade delete), link_type (str: 'elaborates'|'contradicts'|'see-also'|'supports'|'questions'), created_at. stories.title: Note nodes in Viz Knowledge and Learning Path graph modes
stories.phase: V3 Phase 2 - Note Intelligence and Synthesis
stories.description: CONTEXT: After Phase 1, Note nodes exist in Kuzu with WRITTEN_ABOUT, TAG_IS_CONCEPT, and DERIVED_FROM edges. However the Viz tab Knowledge and Learning Path graph modes only query Entity and Document nodes -- Note nodes are invisible to the user in the graph. This story adds a 'Show notes' toggle to the Viz sidebar that overlays Note nodes onto the existing Knowledge graph: each Note node renders as a distinct shape (square vs Entity's circle), sized by outgoing link count, positioned by ForceAtlas2 near the entities it references via WRITTEN_ABOUT edges. Clicking a Note node opens the note detail panel rather than the entity popover. USER JOURNEY: User is viewing the Knowledge graph for 'Domain-Driven Design' -> toggles 'Show notes' -> 12 note nodes appear near the Entity clusters they reference -- 3 blue squares near 'Aggregate Root', 2 near 'Bounded Context' -> user clicks a note node -> note preview panel slides in showing the full content and a 'Open in Notes tab' button. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Viz tab (S112, S135, S136) sidebar and canvas -- adds Note nodes to the existing Sigma.js graph. Note nodes (S163) Kuzu schema used. Note-to-note links (S171) LINKS_TO edges also rendered when 'Show notes' is active. GET /graph/{doc_id} backend must return Note nodes and edges. BACKEND: graph.py get_graph() method: when include_notes=True query param set, run additional Cypher: MATCH (n:Note)-[:WRITTEN_ABOUT|:TAG_IS_CONCEPT]->(e:Entity) WHERE e.id IN (existing_entity_ids) RETURN n.note_id, n.preview, collect(e.id). Also MATCH (n1:Note)-[l:LINKS_TO]->(n2:Note) RETURN n1.note_id, n2.note_id, l.link_type. Append note nodes (type='note', id=note_id, label=first 40 chars of preview, note_count=outgoing_link_count) and WRITTEN_ABOUT / LINKS_TO edges to the graph response. GET /graph/{doc_id} and GET /graph (library-wide): add optional include_notes: bool query param (default false). FRONTEND: Viz sidebar: 'Notes' layer toggle checkbox (below existing Diagrams / Prerequisites / Cross-book toggles). When checked: fetches graph with include_notes=true; adds Note nodes to Graphology graph using a custom square node renderer (reuse the custom WebGL program pattern from DiagramNode hexagon). Note node color: indigo (#6366f1). Size: max(8, sqrt(outgoing_link_count) * 5). On click: opens NotePreviewPanel (new component) anchored to the right side -- shows note preview, tags, collection memberships, 'Open in Notes tab' button. LINKS_TO edges between Note nodes: rendered as dashed indigo lines. WRITES_ABOUT edges (Note -> Entity): thin grey lines. STATES: Toggle on with no notes in Kuzu: no extra nodes appear (graceful empty). Note node click: panel loading state while fetching full note (GET /notes/{id}). Note node with deleted source note: skipped in rendering (note_id not found in GET /notes returns gracefully). TESTS: Unit test: get_graph with include_notes=True returns note nodes with correct entity edge list. Unit test: note with no WRITTEN_ABOUT edges does not appear in graph response (isolated notes excluded). Vitest: Note nodes render with correct color and shape from mock graph fixture. Vitest: note node click opens NotePreviewPanel with correct content.

stories.title: Collection health report: cohesion score, orphaned notes, coverage gaps, stale notes
stories.phase: V3 Phase 2 - Note Intelligence and Synthesis
stories.description: CONTEXT: Collections (S161) organize notes into named groups, but there is no feedback loop telling the user whether a collection is coherent, growing stale, or missing flashcard coverage. The Deck Health Report (S160) provides this for flashcard decks; this story provides the equivalent for note collections. A low-cohesion collection (notes have very different embedding vectors) signals it needs splitting. Orphaned notes (in no collection) signal organizational debt. Notes with no linked flashcards signal study coverage gaps. USER JOURNEY: User opens Notes tab -> clicks gear icon on 'Machine Learning' collection -> Collection Health panel opens: Cohesion 78% (green), 3 orphaned notes in library, 5 notes with no flashcard coverage, 2 notes not edited in 90+ days (stale) -> user clicks 'Move uncovered notes to study queue' -> GenerateFlashcardsDialog pre-populated with those 5 notes -> user generates 10 new cards. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: NoteCollectionModel (S161) is the target entity. FlashcardModel.source_content_hash (S169) used to identify notes already covered. Note embeddings from note_vectors_v2 (S170 dim fix) used for cohesion score. BACKEND: CollectionHealthService in app/services/collection_health.py. CollectionHealthReport TypedDict: {collection_id, collection_name, cohesion_score: float, note_count: int, orphaned_notes: list[str], uncovered_notes: list[{note_id, preview}], stale_notes: list[{note_id, preview, last_updated}], hotspot_tags: list[{tag, count}]}. cohesion_score: fetch vectors for all note_ids in collection from LanceDB (asyncio.to_thread), compute mean pairwise cosine similarity, return as 0-1 float. If < 6 notes: return None (insufficient data). orphaned_notes: SELECT id FROM notes WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT note_id FROM note_collection_members) -- notes belonging to no collection at all (not just this collection). uncovered_notes: notes in this collection whose id has no FlashcardModel row with source='note' and deck=collection.name. stale_notes: notes in this collection with updated_at < now - 90 days. hotspot_tags: top 5 tags by count across notes in this collection. GET /collections/{id}/health: returns CollectionHealthReport. POST /collections/{id}/health/archive-stale: sets a new NoteModel.archived (bool, nullable) column = True for stale notes; returns {archived: int}. Archived notes hidden from default GET /notes list (add WHERE archived IS NOT TRUE filter). NoteModel: add archived (bool default False) column via db_init.py ALTER TABLE. FRONTEND: CollectionTree item: gear icon on hover (alongside the existing rename/delete). Gear click opens CollectionHealthPanel (Sheet or Dialog). Panel sections: Cohesion score pill (green >= 0.7, yellow >= 0.5, red < 0.5); Uncovered Notes list with 'Generate Flashcards' button (opens GenerateFlashcardsDialog pre-populated with those note_ids); Stale Notes list with 'Archive' button; Orphaned Notes count with 'Review' button (filters Notes tab to unassigned notes). STATES: Loading: skeleton sections. No data (< 6 notes): 'Not enough notes to compute cohesion' message. Cohesion unavailable: show dashes. TESTS: Unit test: cohesion_score of 6 identical vectors = 1.0; 6 random unit vectors < 0.5. Unit test: uncovered_notes excludes notes that have a matching deck card. Unit test: stale_notes selects only notes older than 90 days. Unit test: archive-stale sets archived=True and GET /notes excludes them by default.

stories.title: Export: collections as Obsidian-compatible Markdown vault and as Anki deck
stories.phase: V3 Phase 2 - Note Intelligence and Synthesis
stories.description: CONTEXT: Notes and flashcards are currently locked inside Luminary's SQLite database. Users who want to review outside the app (on mobile, in Obsidian, or in Anki) have no path. This story adds two export formats: (1) Obsidian-compatible Markdown vault -- one .md file per note, YAML frontmatter with tags and collection path, [[note_id|title]] links converted to [[note title]] wikilinks, exported as a .zip; (2) Anki deck -- one .apkg file containing all flashcards in a collection (or all note-sourced cards), using the standard Anki note model fields (Front, Back). USER JOURNEY: User opens CollectionTree -> right-click or actions menu on 'Quantum Mechanics' collection -> 'Export' menu with two options: 'Markdown Vault (.zip)' and 'Anki Deck (.apkg)' -> selects Markdown Vault -> browser downloads quantum-mechanics-vault.zip -> unzipping reveals .md files with YAML frontmatter and note content. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: NoteLinkModel (S171) -- [[id|text]] markers in note content converted to Obsidian [[target title]] wikilinks. FlashcardModel (S93, S169) -- Anki export includes cards where deck=collection.name. NoteCollectionModel (S161) -- collection path used as Obsidian folder path in zip. BACKEND: ExportService in app/services/export_service.py. export_collection_markdown(collection_id, db) -> bytes: fetch all notes in collection (including child collections via NoteCollectionMemberModel join). For each note: build YAML frontmatter (tags, collection path as folder list, created_at, source document title if set). Convert note content: replace [[note_id|text]] markers with [[{target_note_preview[:40]}]] Obsidian wikilinks (resolve target by note_id lookup). Write each note as {note_id[:8]}-{slugified-first-line}.md. Zip all files with zipfile.ZipFile in memory. Return bytes. export_collection_anki(collection_id, db) -> bytes: use genanki library (add to pyproject.toml) to build a Deck with id=hash(collection_name), name=collection_name. For each FlashcardModel where deck=collection.name: create genanki.Note(fields=[flashcard.question, flashcard.answer]). Package as .apkg bytes. GET /collections/{id}/export?format=markdown: returns .zip with Content-Disposition attachment. GET /collections/{id}/export?format=anki: returns .apkg with Content-Disposition attachment. FRONTEND: CollectionTree item actions menu (three-dot or right-click): add 'Export' submenu with 'Markdown Vault' and 'Anki Deck' items. On click: trigger browser download via window.location.href = /collections/{id}/export?format=markdown (or anki). Show loading toast while downloading; success toast on completion. STATES: No notes in collection: export returns empty zip / minimal apkg gracefully. No flashcards for Anki export: apkg with 0 cards returned with a warning toast 'No flashcards found for this collection -- generate some first'. TESTS: Unit test: export_collection_markdown for a 3-note collection returns a valid zip with 3 .md files containing correct YAML frontmatter. Unit test: [[id|text]] markers are converted to [[target title]] wikilinks. Unit test: export_collection_anki returns a non-empty bytes object with genanki-valid structure. Unit test: GET /collections/{id}/export?format=markdown returns 200 with Content-Type application/zip.

stories.title: Multi-document notes: cross-book annotations with dual entity edge support
stories.phase: V3 Phase 2 - Note Intelligence and Synthesis
stories.description: CONTEXT: NoteModel.document_id is a single nullable FK. A user comparing a concept across two books (e.g. 'Bounded Context in DDD vs Microservices') cannot formally link their note to both source documents. The DERIVED_FROM edge in Kuzu (S163) is also single-target. This story upgrades notes to support multiple source documents via a NoteSourceModel pivot table, updates the Kuzu graph to emit DERIVED_FROM edges for all source documents, and upgrades the NoteEditorDialog source picker to a multi-select. USER JOURNEY: User is reading both 'Domain-Driven Design' and 'Building Microservices' -> creates a note 'Bounded Context vs Service Boundary: both texts agree on loose coupling but disagree on team ownership' -> in NoteEditorDialog source picker selects both books -> note now has DERIVED_FROM edges to both Document nodes in Kuzu -> GET /notes?document_id={ddd_id} and GET /notes?document_id={ms_id} both return this note -> in Knowledge graph with 'Show notes' (S172) the note appears near entities from both books. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: NoteModel.document_id (S89) is deprecated and nullable -- kept for backward compat but new source pivots stored in NoteSourceModel. Notes as Kuzu nodes (S163) NoteGraphService.upsert_note_node must emit DERIVED_FROM edge for each NoteSourceModel row. GET /notes?document_id= filter must join NoteSourceModel in addition to NoteModel.document_id. NoteEditorDialog (S87, S164, S165) document selector upgraded from single-select to multi-select. BACKEND: NoteSourceModel in models.py: note_id (FK cascade), document_id (FK cascade), added_at. Composite PK (note_id, document_id). db_init.py: CREATE TABLE note_sources. Migration: INSERT INTO note_sources(note_id, document_id, added_at) SELECT id, document_id, created_at FROM notes WHERE document_id IS NOT NULL. NoteGraphService.upsert_note_node: after existing DERIVED_FROM logic for NoteModel.document_id, also query NoteSourceModel rows for this note_id and emit a DERIVED_FROM edge for each. Notes router: POST /notes and PUT/PATCH /notes/{id}: accept source_document_ids (list[str], optional) in request body alongside legacy document_id. On save: sync NoteSourceModel rows (delete rows not in new list, insert new ones), call upsert_note_node. GET /notes?document_id= filter: WHERE notes.document_id = :doc_id OR notes.id IN (SELECT note_id FROM note_sources WHERE document_id = :doc_id). GET /notes/{id} response: add source_document_ids: list[str] field from NoteSourceModel join. FRONTEND: NoteEditorDialog source section: replace single DocumentSelector dropdown with a multi-select DocumentSelector (checkboxes or tags-style, loading from GET /documents). Show selected document titles as chips with X to remove. On save: pass source_document_ids in request body. Reading Journal tab (S148) 'Convert to Note' button: pre-populates document_id as the single source -- still works via legacy path. STATES: Loading document list in multi-select: skeleton. No documents ingested: 'Ingest a book to link notes to it'. TESTS: Unit test: NoteSourceModel rows created for each source_document_id in POST /notes. Unit test: GET /notes?document_id={id} returns notes linked via NoteSourceModel pivot as well as NoteModel.document_id. Unit test: upsert_note_node emits DERIVED_FROM edge for each NoteSourceModel row. Unit test: migration inserts NoteSourceModel rows for all notes with non-null document_id.
